summary: H+H's own reporting says the FY24 spike to 64.1 days was "not reflective of actual performance": a February 2024 cyberattack on the clearinghouse vendor that processes patient statements, insurance eligibility, and claims disrupted the System's ability to post and reconcile payments, artificially inflating the accounts-receivable figure. The drop to 31.8 days in FY25 reflects the System having "mostly rebounded" from that disruption, plus a large one-time payment the System received at the end of June 2025.

sources.label: NYC Health + Hospitals, Fiscal 2024 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Health + Hospitals, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing after rebounding from a FY24 cyberattack that disrupted billing
